operating_state: Operating revenue decreased 43% to $7.1 billion compared to June 2019 quarter due to reduced demand from COVID-19 pandemic. Business showed improvement beginning March 2021 and progressing through June quarter, with recovery expected to continue throughout remainder of year. Pre-tax income was $776 million including $1.5 billion benefit from payroll support program grants.
cost_structure: Total operating expense decreased 39% compared to June 2019 quarter primarily from lower volume-related expenses including fuel and passenger commissions, reduced salaries and related costs, and recognition of payroll support program grants. Significant cost reduction measures were implemented across all operational aspects in response to pandemic impact.
material_events: Entered into Payroll Support Program 3 Agreement receiving approximately $3.1 billion in support consisting of $2.2 billion grants and $890 million unsecured loan. Appointed Daniel Janki as Chief Financial Officer effective July 2021 and promoted John Laughter to Executive Vice President Chief of Operations. Exercised purchase rights for 25 Airbus A321neo aircraft converting to firm orders. Made $1.5 billion contributions to defined benefit pension plans during quarter.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues to significantly impact business operations and financial results. Department of Transportation has authority until March 2022 to require maintenance of scheduled air service to points served before March 2020. Payroll support program conditions include prohibitions on share repurchases and dividends through September 2022.
forward_outlook: Management expects business improvement to continue throughout remainder of 2021 following recovery trend that began in March. Committed to $1.0 billion investment over 10 years for carbon neutrality plan including sustainable aviation fuel and carbon sequestration technologies. Payroll support funds must be used exclusively for employee wages, salaries and benefits with employment restrictions through September 2021.
uncertainty_profile: Payroll support program agreements contain conditions on involuntary layoffs, executive compensation limitations until April 2023, and potential service requirements. Treasury received warrants to acquire approximately 1.9 million shares representing less than 0.3% of outstanding stock. Combined with prior program warrants, total potential dilution represents less than 1.7% of outstanding shares upon exercise.
